experiment: DOMEX-CAT-S469 — FM-27 body-text numerical claim decay hardening
method: Built numerical_claim_scanner.py — extracts numerical claims from lesson body text, checks for @S{NNN} timestamps. Wired into check.sh as NOTICE-level pre-commit guard. Ran baseline audit of all 1063 lessons.
expect: FM-27 UNMITIGATED→MINIMAL with 1 automated advisory layer (pre-commit NOTICE on staged lessons with unstamped numerical claims). Baseline measurement: >90% of claims unstamped.
actual: FM-27 UNMITIGATED→MINIMAL. 1 automated advisory layer wired. Baseline: 4162 claims across 1063 lessons, 0.0% stamped (only L-894 itself has 1 stamped claim). Confirms L-601: prescribed rule with zero voluntary adoption.
diff: Expected >90% unstamped — actual 99.98% (only 1/4162 stamped). Prescription decay even worse than predicted. Creation-time enforcement now active.

data.check_sh_integration: NOTICE-level, between FM-37 and FM-30 guards

false_positive_calibration.method: tested on L-1164, L-1165 — correctly identifies numerical claims, correctly skips lesson IDs and session refs
false_positive_calibration.patterns_excluded: L-NNN, S-NNN, P-NNN, FM-NN, file paths, line numbers

candidates.name: False instrument rate (33% at N>500)
candidates.evidence: L-1165 historian synthesis: 3/9 experiments (33%) had wrong measurement criterion. Extends L-1091 sign-stripping pattern to systematic.

candidates.name: Discovery ratio baseline contamination
candidates.evidence: L-1164: SWARMER1 54:1 was artifact — DOMEX-only ratio is 9.2:1. Non-experimental L2 lessons inflate confirmation count by default.
